pattern: Eating one-by-one is never worth doing more than once or twice before a division becomes available, so a memoized recursion on n that only ever branches into n/2 and n/3 (after eating off the remainder) collapses an exponential state space down to O(log n) distinct subproblems.
hints: Eating oranges one at a time n times is always dominated by using the divide-by-2 or divide-by-3 rule as soon as possible, so a good recursion should jump straight to n // 2 and n // 3 rather than counting down one by one.
Define days(n) = 1 + min(days(n // 2) plus the (n % 2) single-orange days needed to get there, days(n // 3) plus the (n % 3) single-orange days needed to get there), with days(0) = 0.
Memoize on n -- even though n can be up to 2 * 10^9, repeatedly dividing by 2 or 3 only ever produces O(log n) distinct values, so a dictionary cache keeps this fast.

edge_inputs.description: ten oranges, mixing all three actions
six oranges, divides evenly by both 2 and 3
zero oranges left, no days needed
a power of two, favors repeated halving
a power of three, favors the divide-by-three rule

stress.description: random large n values across the full range, including some near powers of 2 and 3
